details: Dependency-confusion attack: @higherlogic/ocfe@99.9.1 is a stub package (empty index.js) that declares a dependency on "ltidisafe" resolved from an external Google Cloud Storage URL (hxxps://ltidi[.]storage[.]googleapis[.]com/depenconf/ltidisafe-3[.]1[.]5[.]tgz). The scoped name mimics an internal/private package and the high version (99.9.1) out-ranks any legitimate internal version. When installed, npm fetches the external tarball, delivering the real payload through dependency resolution. The external tarball at ltidi[.]storage[.]googleapis[.]com/depenconf/ltidisafe-3.1.5.tgz is the actual malicious component. Dependency-confusion package @higherlogic/ocfe at version 99.9.1. The package declares a dependency on a remote tarball hosted at ltidi[.]storage[.]googleapis[.]com (ltidisafe-3.1.5.tgz), which is fetched and installed at runtime. The package has no repository, no description, and a minimal index.js stub, consistent with a remote-code-execution dropper pattern.

cwes.name: Inclusion of Functionality from Untrusted Control Sphere
